comment: Flow_Parse (§5.5). Reads the PDF_MAPPER_ENABLED Dataverse env-var (READ, never DEFINE), branches: enabled -> call the cedocumentmapper_v2 parser connector (ParseDocument) -> write the 12 EVA fields as staff-review pre-fill (never auto-final) + provenance; disabled -> audit skip, leave fields for manual entry. Phase-5 OCR fallback (ADDITIVE, GATED on cr1bd_OCR_SCANNED_PDF_ENABLED, default OFF): after the text parse, when the extraction came back ~empty (image-only/scanned PDF) AND the OCR gate reads true, call OcrPdf via the cr1bd_ocr connector and coalesce the OCR extraction OVER the (empty) text parse before prefill. Gate-off / non-empty parse leaves the original path byte-for-byte unchanged. [BUILD] flow; parser + OCR host Function deploys are [DEPLOY-WITH-LOGIN]; flipping the OCR gate is [RESERVED-FOR-USER].

Get_gate_definition.comment: Read the CURRENT value (coalesce value-row override, else definition default). Flows READ gates; never write environmentvariablevalue/definition.

Set_gate_PDF_MAPPER_ENABLED.comment: Env-var values are always strings in Dataverse, even Boolean type -> equals(toLower(...),'true'). M1 default true.

Get_ocr_gate_definition.comment: Phase-5 OCR fallback gate read (READ, never DEFINE; same idiom as Get_gate_definition). Coalesces the value-row override over the definition default; default 'false' so the fallback is dark until the operator flips it.

Set_gate_OCR_SCANNED_PDF_ENABLED.comment: Env-var values are always strings in Dataverse, even Boolean type -> equals(toLower(...),'true'). Phase-5 default false (the OCR fallback ships dark).

Call_parser.comment: body/document is the RAW base64 string (@triggerBody()?['instructionBytesB64']) — NOT @base64ToBinary(...). The connector param is a plain string; base64ToBinary feeds the gateway BINARY -> it 400s (proven 2026-06-20: test34 -> 400 -> Exceptions; the SAME doc posts 200 directly to /api/parse). The gateway double-encodes the raw string and the parser's TOLERANT _decode_document peels the redundant layer. See memory powerplatform-connector-base64-double-encode. ParseDocument returns { extraction:{12 EVA fields}, vrm, reference, issues, contract_version }. vrm/reference are Case-identity fields, NOT EVA payload fields.

If_extraction_empty_and_ocr_enabled.comment: Phase-5 OCR fallback (ADDITIVE, GATED). Fires ONLY when the first text-based parse came back ~empty (no vrm/reference and none of the lead extraction fields work_provider/vehicle_model/claimant_name) AND cr1bd_OCR_SCANNED_PDF_ENABLED reads true — the image-only/scanned-PDF case. Calls OcrPdf via cr1bd_ocr (the OCR host re-runs OCR then the same engine extraction) and Prefill_eva_fields below coalesces the OCR result OVER the (empty) text parse, so a text-rich doc is untouched and the gate-off path is identical to before. Empty extraction + gate-off (or a non-empty text parse) -> this If is skipped and Prefill_eva_fields uses the text parse exactly as today.

Call_OcrPdf.comment: body/document is the RAW base64 string (NOT @base64ToBinary) — same Power Platform gateway double-encode landmine as the parser; the OCR host's tolerant decode peels the redundant layer (memory powerplatform-connector-base64-double-encode). OcrPdf returns the SAME shape as ParseDocument { extraction:{12 EVA fields}, vrm, reference, ocr_text, ocr_provider, issues, contract_version } so the prefill pointer paths are identical. Invoked ONLY as the empty-extraction fallback (gated upstream).

Prefill_eva_fields.comment: 12 EVA fields written as pre-fill for staff review (never auto-final). Each value coalesces the OCR-fallback result (body('Call_OcrPdf')) OVER the text parse (body('Call_parser')) — when the OCR If was Skipped (gate off, or the text parse was non-empty) body('Call_OcrPdf') is null and the coalesce falls back to the text parse exactly as before, so the original path is unchanged. The mileage FieldLevelProvenance row (sourceType=pdf_extraction, reviewState=needs_review, sourceLabel='From instructions') is written by Write_mileage_provenance below — the marker the documentation-first source-priority logic relies on. (S1: mileage is the first provenance field this flow writes; the other 11 fields get their rows when the per-field provenance writer is generalised. ADR-0006 logic already lives in enrich.definition.json's Set_documentHasMileage.) Re-parse overwrites only pdf_extraction-sourced fields still in needs_review. runAfter the OCR If on Succeeded+Skipped so the prefill always runs whether or not the fallback fired.

Write_mileage_provenance.comment: MARKER WRITER (S1): when the parser extracted a mileage, write a FieldLevelProvenance row stamping the documented/instructions source. cr1bd_fieldname uses the camelCase EVA key 'mileage' (the EVA_FIELD_ORDER key the adapter joins on), NOT the Case column cr1bd_evamileage. This is the row that makes the 'From instructions' provenance badge appear and proves document mileage won (enrich.definition.json fills the DVSA estimate ONLY when this never ran). Mirrors the Audit_parser_called CreateRecord idiom (same connection/host); Case lookup via cr1bd_Caseid@odata.bind.

Create_mileage_provenance.comment: cr1bd_sourcetype=100000001 (pdf_extraction, per dataverse/choicesets/field-provenance-source-type.json); cr1bd_reviewstate=100000001 (needs_review, per dataverse/choicesets/review-state.json). Both READ from the real artifacts, not guessed.

parameters.item/cr1bd_after: @concat('parsed; contract_version=', coalesce(body('Call_OcrPdf')?['contract_version'], body('Call_parser')?['contract_version'], 'unknown'), if(equals(coalesce(outputs('Call_OcrPdf')?['statusCode'], 0), 200), concat('; OCR fallback used (', coalesce(body('Call_OcrPdf')?['ocr_provider'], 'unknown'), ')'), ''))

Audit_parser_called.comment: Records the parse. When the empty-extraction OCR fallback fired (Call_OcrPdf returned 200) the after-text notes the OCR engine that ran; outputs('Call_OcrPdf') is null-safe (coalesce -> 0) when the fallback was skipped.

parameters.item/cr1bd_after: skipped: PDF_MAPPER_ENABLED=false - 12 fields left for manual entry

@concat('parser failed: ', string(coalesce(outputs('Call_parser')?['statusCode'], 'unknown')), ' (', coalesce(body('Call_parser')?['message'], 'no details'), ') - left at needs_review; see run history')

Audit_parser_failed.comment: On Function 5xx/timeout the parser branch fails; audit parser_failed and continue (enrichment/status downstream handle needs_review).

Respond_to_parent.comment: Child-flow return action (required to be callable via Run-a-Child-Flow; mirrors classify-persist's Respond_to_parent / 'Respond to a PowerApp or flow'). Echoes the instruction bytes+name the parent passed in plus a parsed flag and the parser contract_version. parsed is true when the gate is on AND either the text parse OR the OCR fallback returned 200; ocrFallbackUsed reports whether the empty-extraction OCR fallback ran. runAfter the gate branch on ALL outcomes (Succeeded/Skipped = ran or gate off; Failed/TimedOut absorbed by Audit_parser_failed) so the child ALWAYS returns to the parent even when the parser Function 5xxs. outputs('Call_parser')/outputs('Call_OcrPdf') are null-safe (coalesce -> 0) when the respective branch was skipped (gate off / fallback not taken).
